Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log of a loan application process The log contains several cases C001 to C005 with activities from ApplicationReceived to FinalDecision We are asked to identify where and how bias manifests in this process which attributes and adjustments favor certain groups and how that might influence fairness and equity

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We see that for cases C001 and C004 they are LocalResident TRUE and have CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club In both during PreliminaryScoring there is a ScoreAdjustment of plus 10 Community That means they get a 10 point bonus because they belong to that community group

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The PreliminaryScore is adjusted upward by 10 For C001 PreliminaryScore 710 becomes 720 adjusted For C004 690 becomes 700 The final decisions for both are Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For cases C002 C003 C005 they have CommunityGroup None C002 LocalResident TRUE C003 LocalResident FALSE C005 LocalResident FALSE For these ScoreAdjustment is 0 No bonus

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now look at decisions C001 Approved with adjusted score 720 C002 Approved with score 720 no adjustment but same final score as C001 C003 Rejected with score 715 C004 Approved with adjusted score 700 C005 Approved with score 740

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So what might be the threshold for approval Possibly a score of 700 or above leads to approval

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But note C003 has score 715 and is rejected Why Possibly because C003 is not a local resident LocalResident FALSE That might be a factor Also C005 is not a local resident but has score 740 and is approved

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So maybe there is a cutoff for non locals at something above 715 say 720 or 730 But C001 and C002 are locals with 720 and 720 approved C004 local with 700 approved So locals seem to be approved at 700 or above Non locals C003 at 715 rejected C005 at 740 approved So it appears that non locals require a higher score to be approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That could be a bias based on local residency

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also there is a community group bonus only applicants from Highland Civic Darts Club get a plus 10 adjustment That is a direct favoritism towards members of that specific community group

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This could be a form of bias favoring individuals affiliated with that group

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: We can also see that the manual review is done by different reviewers Could there be bias in manual review The log does not show that the reviewer changes the score they just review

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the bias is embedded in the automated scoring engine it adds 10 points for community group membership

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possibly the rules engine applies different thresholds based on LocalResident status

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the biases identified Community Group Bias A plus 10 score adjustment is given to applicants belonging to the Highland Civic Darts Club This directly advantages members of that club potentially at the expense of others with similar creditworthiness but without that affiliation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Local Residency Bias It appears that local residents are approved at a lower score threshold than non residents C004 local approved at 700 while C003 non local rejected at 715 This suggests that the decision threshold is lower for locals

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This disadvantages non local applicants who need a higher credit score to get approved

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Individuals who are not local residents or not part of the favored community group may be denied loans even if their underlying creditworthiness as reflected by preliminary score is similar or even higher than those who get approved due to adjustments or lower thresholds This reduces fairness and equity

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The adjustments based on community affiliation and geographic residency may not be relevant to credit risk and could be discriminatory
